eddy_skin.pulsed_dc_note: single-time-constant illustrative model; real settling is multi-mode

closed_form_init.note: Eigenvalue ratio 1:1:4 confirms the derivation; clean recovery is machine-precision (Ch.23 §23.5).

dual_coil_obs.note: Roll about the common axis is unobservable at 0 deg (sigma_min~0) and maximal at 90 deg; scales ~ sin(theta) (Ch.13 §13.3).

crlb_6dof.note: Marginalized position CRLB (position block of the full 6x6 F^-1) vs naive (inverse of the F_pp block alone, ignoring orientation). The ratio is the coupling penalty from estimating unknown orientation (Schur complement) and is a pose-INVARIANT constant ~2.952 (variance ~8.7x) for the co-located triad/triad geometry: the honest 6-DOF position CRLB is ~3x worse than an orientation-known bound. Orientation CRLB scales as z^3.0 (one power less than position's z^4, because orientation reads the field itself while position reads its gradient).

deep_volume.note: CRLB sigma_pos ~ 1/m_t (exponent ~-1) at fixed range, so usable depth z_max(tau) ~ m_t^0.25: a 16x moment buys only ~2x depth. Doubling usable depth needs ~16x moment (~16x power -> Ch.37 thermal wall) -> multi-generator (Ch.9.8) is the practical deep-volume/bariatric route, not brute moment.

distortion_flag_roc.note: A single-sensor residual (redundant 9>6 measurement, Ch.27) detects only the part of distortion INCONSISTENT with the dipole model; distortion that mimics a 6-DOF pose shift inflates error but not the residual. The detection margin (eta_danger - eta_flag) is therefore GEOMETRY-DEPENDENT and can be NEGATIVE (flag fires AFTER the error is dangerous). Conclusion: a single residual flag is necessary but NOT sufficient -> independent redundancy (witness sensor Ch.27.3, 2nd generator Ch.9.8, fusion Ch.21.9) is required, and flag latency/false-alarm MUST be measured per the proposed Ch.33 §33.9 benchmark, not assumed.
